name: Python
creator: Guido van Rossum
description: High-level, general-purpose language known for readability and a large ecosystem.

name: JavaScript
creator: Brendan Eich
description: The scripting language of the web, now widely used server-side via Node.js.

name: TypeScript
creator: Anders Hejlsberg (Microsoft)
description: Typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to plain JS.

name: Rust
creator: Graydon Hoare (Mozilla)
description: Systems language focused on memory safety without a garbage collector.

name: Go
creator: Robert Griesemer, Rob Pike, Ken Thompson (Google)
description: Statically typed, compiled language designed for simplicity and fast builds.

name: C
creator: Dennis Ritchie (Bell Labs)
description: Low-level systems language foundational to most modern operating systems.

name: C++
creator: Bjarne Stroustrup
description: Extension of C with object-oriented features, used in games, engines and systems.

name: C#
creator: Anders Hejlsberg (Microsoft)
description: Modern, multi-paradigm language on the .NET runtime, popular for game dev with Unity.

name: Java
creator: James Gosling (Sun Microsystems)
description: Write-once-run-anywhere JVM language widely used in enterprise and Android.

name: Kotlin
creator: JetBrains
description: Modern JVM language, officially preferred for Android development.

name: Swift
creator: Chris Lattner (Apple)
description: Apple's modern language for iOS, macOS, and beyond.

name: Ruby
creator: Yukihiro Matsumoto
description: Expressive, dynamic language known for Rails and developer happiness.

name: PHP
creator: Rasmus Lerdorf
description: Server-side scripting language powering a large share of the web (WordPress, Laravel).

name: Scala
creator: Martin Odersky
description: JVM language combining OOP and FP, used in big data (Spark) and distributed systems.

name: Haskell
creator: Committee (Simon Peyton Jones et al.)
description: Purely functional language with strong static typing and lazy evaluation.

name: Lua
creator: PUC-Rio
description: Lightweight embeddable scripting language popular in games and config (Neovim, Roblox).

name: R
creator: Ross Ihaka, Robert Gentleman
description: Statistical computing language essential for data science and bioinformatics.

name: Julia
creator: Jeff Bezanson, Stefan Karpinski, Viral B. Shah, Alan Edelman
description: High-performance numerical computing language with Python-like syntax.

name: Elixir
creator: José Valim
description: Functional language on the Erlang BEAM VM, great for scalable real-time systems.

name: Erlang
creator: Joe Armstrong (Ericsson)
description: Battle-tested language for fault-tolerant distributed systems (WhatsApp, RabbitMQ).

name: Zig
creator: Andrew Kelley
description: Low-level systems language aiming to replace C with explicit control over memory.

name: Nim
creator: Andreas Rumpf
description: Statically typed compiled language with Python-like syntax and C-like performance.

name: Dart
creator: Lars Bak, Kasper Lund (Google)
description: Client-optimised language powering Flutter for cross-platform UI.

name: SQL
creator: Donald D. Chamberlin, Raymond F. Boyce (IBM)
description: Standard language for relational database management and queries.

name: Bash
creator: Brian Fox (GNU Project)
description: The Bourne Again SHell — the default shell on most Linux systems.

name: Perl
creator: Larry Wall
description: High-level scripting language known for text processing and CGI web scripting.

name: OCaml
creator: INRIA
description: Industrial-strength functional language with type inference, used in finance and compilers.

name: F#
creator: Don Syme (Microsoft)
description: Functional-first .NET language strongly influenced by OCaml.

name: Clojure
creator: Rich Hickey
description: Modern Lisp dialect on the JVM, emphasising immutability and concurrency.

name: WebAssembly
creator: W3C WebAssembly Community Group
description: Binary instruction format enabling near-native performance in web browsers.
